ddls:
CREATE TABLE "suburbs" (
  "suburb_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each suburb */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the suburb */,
  "postcode" TEXT /* Postcode of the suburb */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region the suburb belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the suburb */,
  "long_c" REAL /* Longitude of the suburb center */,
  "lat_c" REAL /* Latitude of the suburb center */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the suburb in square kilometers */,
  "elevation_m" REAL /* Average elevation of the suburb in meters */,
  "lga_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the local government area (LGA) the suburb belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the suburb record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the suburb record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suburb_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_suburbs_lga_id FOREIGN KEY ("lga_id") REFERENCES local_government_areas ("lga_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_suburbs_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "state" TEXT /* State in which the region is located */,
  "region_type" TEXT /* Type of the region (e.g., metropolitan, rural) */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the region in square kilometers */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the region (population per square kilometer) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the region record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the region record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "suburb_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the suburb the data pertains to */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., 0-14, 15-24, 25-64, 65+) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender category (e.g., Male, Female, Other) */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of individuals in the specified category */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the demographic data */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the demographic record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the demographic record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_suburb_id FOREIGN KEY ("suburb_id") REFERENCES suburbs ("suburb_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "local_government_areas" (
  "lga_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each local government area */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the local government area */,
  "state" TEXT /* State in which the local government area is located */,
  "council_type" TEXT /* Type of council (e.g., City, Shire) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the LGA */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the LGA in square kilometers */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the LGA record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the LGA record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lga_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population statistic record */,
  "suburb_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the suburb the statistics belong to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the population data */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population count for that year */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the population statistic record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the population statistic record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_statistics_suburb_id FOREIGN KEY ("suburb_id") REFERENCES suburbs ("suburb_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "distance_calculations" (
  "distance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each distance record */,
  "suburb_id_1" INTEGER /* Reference to the first suburb */,
  "suburb_id_2" INTEGER /* Reference to the second suburb */,
  "distance" REAL /* Calculated distance between the two suburbs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the distance calculation record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the distance calculation record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_distance_calculations_suburb_id_1 FOREIGN KEY ("suburb_id_1") REFERENCES suburbs ("suburb_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_distance_calculations_suburb_id_2 FOREIGN KEY ("suburb_id_2") REFERENCES suburbs ("suburb_id")
)
